Similarly, the average particle size of 9e-loaded PLGA NP (9e/NP) was measured as 222.0 ± 12.490 nm
(ﬁgure 5(c)), the PDI value was 0.247 ± 0.055, and the zeta potential was −12.4 ± 1.42 mV (ﬁgure 5(d)). The
average particle size of empty NP was 181.6 ± 3.306 nm and increased to 216.1 ± 8.944 nm in 9c/NP and
222.0 ± 12.490 in 9e/NP after loading of compounds 9c and 9e. The reason behind the negative zeta potential
value exhibited by all synthesized nanoparticles is attributed to the existence of ionized carboxyl groups
originated from acid-terminated PLGA on the nanoparticles’ surface, as well as the non-ionic PVA utilized as an
emulsiﬁer throughout the synthesis process.

the peaks observed at 3061 and 3017 cm
−1 can be attributed to aromatic C-H stretching vibrations, whereas the

−1 corresponds to the rocking vibration of C–N bonds. The spectrum of 9c/NP (ﬁgure 6(a)) reveals a
noteworthy reduction in the intensity of the primary 9c peaks, accompanied by the prominence of distinct peaks
associated with PLGA. This observation strongly suggests that 9c has been successfully encapsulated within
PLGA nanoparticles. Nevertheless, the FT-IR spectrum of 9c/NP reveals a minimal presence of 9c, as indicated

by the low intensity peaks observed at 1692 cm
−1 and 1264 cm
−1. This suggests that only a small quantity of 9c is
adsorbed onto the surface of 9c/NP. The FT-IR spectrum of compound 9e (ﬁgure 6(b)) displays peaks at

3063 cm
−1 which correspond to aromatic C-H stretching vibrations, while the peaks at 1691 cm
−1 suggest C=O

stretching vibrations. In addition, the peaks at 1465 and 1387 cm
−1 belong to aliphatic C-H in-plane bending,

and the peak at 1263 cm
−1 belongs to C-N rocking vibration. The FT-IR spectrum of 9e/NP (ﬁgure 6(b)) reveals
that 9e is encapsulated in PLGA nanoparticles due to a notable decrease in the strength of the primary 9e peaks
and the predominance of PLGA characteristic peaks in the 9e/NP spectrum. However, the presence of peaks
belonging to compound 9e in the FT-IR spectrum of 9e/NP indicates that only a small amount of 9e is adsorbed
to the nanoparticle surface (similar to that of 9c/NP).
